category_title: Students who exhibit physical distress and avoidance behaviors (crying, falling to floor) in loud environments
minimal_context: The student and tutor are practicing catching a ball while standing on a spot in a busy environment with other kids and noises.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's check the picture schedule to see what is next.

STUDENT: Catch. (Gets the picture schedule out, then stands on a spot)

TUTOR: Hands out, look at the ball.

STUDENT: (Catches with two hands, then squeezes the ball)

TUTOR: Let's try another one. Hands out.

STUDENT: (Looking everywhere, easily distracted by the million noises and people coming in and out)

options.A: Move the student into a position where they are less distracted and not seeing the other people.
options.B: Introduce new motivating items to capture the student's attention during the catching activity in the busy environment.
options.C: Instruct the student to play with any material available in the busy environment instead of the ball.
options.D: Use a 'request for clarification' to ask the student what they are looking at in the busy environment.
